filters: Data must reflect a good faith effort by an organization to provide accurate responses.
Organizations must report complete quarters that precede non-renewal or termination of the plan (PBP) or contract.
If a contract terminates on May 31, the organization must report Quarter 1 (1/1-3/31) for that year.
If a contract non-renews on December 31, the organization must report all quarters for that year.
Organizations should include all 800 series plans.
Employer/Union Direct Contracts should report this section, regardless of organization type.
Organization determinations and reconsiderations should be included if the request is consistent with applicable regulations.
Decisions to extend or continue coverage of the same item or service (e.g., physical therapy) should be reported as separate, fully favorable organization determinations.
If an organization determination includes more than one service, all multiple line items of the decision must be included as separate entries, sharing the same organization determination number.
Requests submitted by staff of a physician’s office on behalf of the physician should be reported as contract or non-contract provider, as appropriate.

The provider’s NPI must be reported on a service decision when the request is submitted by (or on behalf of) a provider, or when an enrollee submits a service request and identifies a provider had ordered the requested item/service/Part B drug or the enrollee requested to receive it from a specific provider.
Report completed organization determinations and reconsiderations (i.e., all required notification has occurred) during the reporting period, regardless of when the request was received.
Report organization determinations or reconsiderations where a substantive decision has been made and processed in accordance with 42 C.F.R. Part 422, Subpart M.

Report a denial of a Medicare request for coverage (payment or provision) of an item or service as either partially favorable or adverse, regardless of whether Medicaid payment or provision is ultimately provided.

Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) that are applicable integrated plans (42 CFR § 422.561) should report a request for a Medicare item or service based on the outcome of applying both Medicare and Medicaid coverage criteria.
Report denials based on exhaustion of Medicare benefits.
Report organization determinations that involve hospital discharges and transfers to post-acute care settings.
Include all requests for supplemental services that meet the criteria defined in 42 CFR § 422.100(c)(2).
business_rules: Data must be submitted via HPMS.
Compliance with reporting requirements is a contractual obligation of all MA organizations.
Data must be accurate and submitted in a timely manner.
Data submissions are due by 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time on the reporting deadline.
CMS tracks resubmissions, including those made after the deadline.
Data is expected to be accurate on the date of submission.
Organizations should store revised data and follow record retention requirements, maintaining documentation supporting reported data.
Organizations should report data based on their interpretation of the Service Level Data Collection and Technical Specifications and be able to support their decisions.
HPMS will not allow entry of '>', '<', ';', or decimals in any data entry field or uploaded file.
Organizations must not submit 'placeholder' data (e.g., '99999').
Numeric fields do not allow characters.
Where multiple options exist, a single selection must be made.
CMS generally does not grant extensions to reporting deadlines.
Any assumptions made for timely data submission must be fully documented and defensible under audit.
A formal 'Request Resubmission' must be submitted via the HPMS Plan Reporting Module (PRM) for corrections after a reporting deadline has passed.
Approval for resubmission requests is valid for 7 days from CMS review and approval.
Organizations should not submit resubmission requests until data is available for submission.
Late data submissions (after the deadline) may not be incorporated into CMS data analyses and reporting.
HPMS will not allow resubmission of data identical to the original submission.
Corrections are appropriate for errors made at the original submission date or as indicated by CMS.
A plan can only initiate a resubmission request after the original reporting deadline has expired.
If CMS revises technical specifications, plans must incorporate changes starting the reporting period following the publication date (e.g., Q2 data reflects March updates).
Validation checks should be performed by each organization prior to data submission.
Contracts should validate that the reconsideration 'date request received' field is later than or equal to the 'date of decision' field for the organization determination.
Date Request Received (Initial Determinations - Coverage Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date of Decision (Initial Determinations - Coverage Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss.
Date Claim Received (Initial Determinations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date of Decision (Initial Determinations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Start Date of Service (Initial Determinations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
End Date of Service (Initial Determinations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date Request Received (Reconsiderations - Coverage Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date of Decision (Reconsiderations - Coverage Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss.
Date Request Received (Reconsiderations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Date of Decision (Reconsiderations - Payment Decisions) format: YYYY-MM-DD.
exclusions: Medicare-Medicaid Plans (MMPs) due to transition of Financial Alignment Initiative demonstrations to D-SNP models
Reopenings requested or completed by the IRE, Administrative Law Judge (ALJ), or Appeals Council
Duplicate payment requests concerning the same service or item
Payment requests returned to a provider/supplier where a substantive decision (fully favorable, partially favorable or adverse) has not been made (e.g., incomplete forms, clerical errors)
Part B drugs paid or denied at the pharmacy and point-of-sale Part B drug claim rejections (unless the plan subsequently processes an organization determination)
Quality Improvement Organization (QIO) review of an individual’s request to continue Medicare-covered services (e.g., SNF stay) and any related claims/requests based on such QIO decision

Services only covered under the plan’s Medicaid benefits and never covered by Medicare and not covered by the MA plan as a supplemental Medicare benefit (e.g., Medicaid home-and community-based long-term services and supports)
Plan decisions regarding a request to vacate a dismissal
notes: CMS intends to conduct a voluntary pilot in 2026 with a select number of MA plans.
Pilot participants will have six months to prepare for the first data submission, estimated in late 2026.
CMS anticipates expanding the data collection to all plans in 2027 after gathering lessons learned from the pilot.
The Technical Specifications are subject to change during pilot implementation.
Technical specifications define data elements and provide information on how CMS will review and analyze data to ensure accuracy.
Detailed instructions for resubmissions are available in the HPMS Plan Reporting Module User Guide.
Questions specific to the Service Level Data Collection can be sent to https://appeals.lmi.org. Immediate responses may not always be possible.
For technical assistance with file formats and uploads, contact the HPMS help desk at 1-800-220-2028 or hpms@cms.hhs.gov.
Definition: Adverse Decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was denied in whole.
Definition: Clean Claim (42 CFR §422.500(b)) is a claim without defect, impropriety, or lack of required documentation, conforming to Original Medicare clean claim requirements.
Definition: Fully Favorable decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was covered in whole.
Definition: Internal Coverage Criteria refers to an MA organization’s established coverage criteria as defined in 42 CFR § 422.101(b)(6).

Definition: Organization Determination is a plan’s response to a request for coverage (payment or provision) of an item, service, or Part B drug, including auto-adjudicated claims, service authorizations (prior-approvals, concurrent authorizations), requests to continue previously authorized treatment, and reduction/discontinuation of services. It includes pre-service requests from enrollees, representatives, contract/non-contract providers, and claims payment requests.
Definition: Concurrent Authorization is a coverage decision made contemporaneously to when an enrollee is receiving the item/service/Part B drug, without a prior authorization decision.
Definition: Partially Favorable decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was partially covered (e.g., some line items paid, or fewer services authorized than requested).
Definition: Prior Approval refers to coverage decisions made before provision of the item/service/Part B drug, including both required prior authorization requests and voluntary pre-service requests.
